title: Java Microservice Memory Leak Causing OOM Crashes
symptoms: Java heap usage growing linearly from 2GB to 8GB over 6-hour cycles
OOM killer terminating the Java process repeatedly
HTTP connection pool showing 4,000+ active connections (expected max: 200)
Increasing garbage collection pause times reaching 30+ seconds
Order processing latency spikes during GC pauses and restarts
root_cause: HTTP connection pool leak in Apache HttpClient where connections were not released back to the pool when upstream service returned timeout exceptions
resolution: Deployed hotfix to properly close HTTP connections in the finally block of the upstream call handler, added connection pool idle timeout configuration, and increased heap dump collection for future diagnosis
preventive_actions: Add JVM heap usage trend alerting with 6-hour moving average
Implement HTTP connection pool monitoring with dashboards
Add automated heap dump capture when heap exceeds 80%
Schedule quarterly code review of connection management patterns
Add connection leak detection in integration test suite

timeline.event: First OOM kill event on order-svc-prod-03
James Wright begins investigation, notices recurring OOM pattern
Heap dump analysis reveals HTTP connection objects consuming 70% of heap
Root cause identified as connection leak in timeout handling path
Hotfix developed and tested in staging environment
Hotfix deployed to production, connection pool stabilizes
Monitoring confirms heap usage stable at 2.5GB, incident resolved

embedding_summary: Java microservice memory leak in the Order Processing service caused by HTTP connection pool leak in Apache HttpClient. Connections were not released on upstream timeouts, causing heap growth from 2GB to 8GB over 6-hour cycles. Resolved by deploying hotfix to properly close connections in finally blocks.
